rationale: No qualitative new developments directly affecting Poland this cycle; Iran deal momentum sustains energy price hopes but remains unsigned, and the Russia-Ukraine war continues as background.

top_events.title: Iran signals Hormuz reopening tied to peace deal
top_events.summary: Iran reaffirms that a deal would reopen Hormuz, sustaining hopes for lower energy prices in Europe. No new qualitative step — deal still unsigned, momentum continues from prior cycle.

top_events.title: Trump seeks 'off-ramp' from Iran war
top_events.summary: US diplomatic signaling toward an Iran deal could reduce geopolitical risk premium on energy, indirectly benefiting Polish consumers and industry through lower gas/oil prices.

top_events.title: China arrests US academic for espionage
top_events.summary: Rising US-China tensions could have indirect EU trade and tech implications, but no direct impact on Poland's daily life this cycle.
